title: machaeranthera kingii
intro: Machaeranthera kingii, also known as King's Lomatium, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States, belonging to the sunflower family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's roots and leaves contain isobutyamides, which have been found to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, providing various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Machaeranthera kingii can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a valuable addition to traditional herbal medicine.
